question_text: How does the strength of a magnetic field change with distance from a magnet?

options.label: A
options.text: It stays the same no matter how far away you are

options.label: B
options.text: It gets stronger the further away you go

options.label: C
options.text: It gets weaker the further away you are

options.label: D
options.text: It only exists at the poles

correct_answer: It gets weaker the further away you are
explanation: Magnetic field strength decreases with distance from a magnet. This is why magnetic effects are strongest at the poles and near the magnet's surface, but become weaker as you move further away. You can observe this by noticing that a magnet can only attract objects that are close enough to it.
